summary: Backend regression for the LangGraph migration of the Marketing OS canonical 5-role chain. Verified (a) the new marketing_os_graph module compiles and conditional edges resolve, (b) the canonical run/stream SSE flow emits the full vocabulary in order (os_started → agent_started → agent_done × N → summarizing → complete → os_persisted) and persists framework='langgraph', (c) the skip_distribution conditional edge bypasses Kai on empty-platforms campaigns, (d) all pre-existing /api/marketing-os/* endpoints still respond, and (e) the legacy /api/ai/agent/convene/stream regression still streams.
critical_code_review_comments: marketing_os_graph.py is well-structured (~570 lines, single responsibility). Lazy singleton + reset hook is clean.
Per-run asyncio.Queue registry (_RUN_QUEUES) is unbounded by design but cleaned up in `finally` — fine for the expected concurrency. Consider a max-size cap if you ever expose unauthenticated runs.

MongoDBSaver fallback to MemorySaver on ping failure is good — but the fallback is silent (just logger.warning). Consider exposing a /api/marketing-os/health flag so ops can detect when checkpointing has been silently downgraded.
_route_after_distribution is a no-op router (always returns 'summariser'); using a plain add_edge('distribution', 'summariser') would be slightly cheaper and clearer, but functionally identical.

marketing_os.py line 273 — `campaign_id = camp.get('name') and payload.campaign_id` is a subtle bug-shaped expression: it works only because every persisted campaign has a non-empty name. Recommend `campaign_id = payload.campaign_id` directly since the find_one already returned a row.

success_rate.backend: 100% (49/49 executed tests across the three suites passed; 2 unrelated SEO-trend tests skipped due to missing seed data)

test_credentials: Test user TOKEN=test_session_1779636592168, USER_ID=user_test1779636592168 (read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Plan comped to 'growth' before each LLM-touching test.
seed_data_creation: Created a transient TEST_LG_empty_<uuid> campaign with platforms=[] to drive the skip_distribution conditional-edge test. Not cleaned up (test data only).
context_for_next_testing_agent: LangGraph migration shipped cleanly. The canonical chain runs via marketing_os_graph.run_os_graph(); SSE shape unchanged so frontend was not touched. New marketing_os_runs rows carry `framework: 'langgraph'` and `skip_distribution` boolean — guard older rows that lack these. If checkpointer-related Mongo issues appear, marketing_os_graph._make_checkpointer() silently falls back to MemorySaver (logger.warning only).

verified_observations.canonical_happy_path: test_short_run_emits_canonical_events_and_persists PASSED — full vocabulary emitted in order; persisted run has status=completed, transcript len >=1, non-empty summary.
verified_observations.skip_distribution_edge: Created campaign with platforms=[]; os_started.skip_distribution=true; SSE stream contained NO agent_started/agent_done for kai; persisted row has framework='langgraph' and skip_distribution=true; run completed.
verified_observations.framework_marker: test_persisted_run_marked_langgraph PASSED — latest run carries framework='langgraph'.
verified_observations.convene_regression: POST /api/ai/agent/convene/stream still streams agent_started/agent_done/complete (uses `message` field, not `brief`).
verified_observations.graph_smoke_tests: All 5 TestLangGraphOrchestrator tests pass (compile, conditional edges, role-id parity, marker).
